title: Sanitizers, Static Analysis, and Fuzzing · Object-Lifetime Lens: Asan Lsan Evidence
summary: A security parser needs fast local feedback, nightly deep instrumentation, adversarial generation, and reproducible findings. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem asan lsan evidence increment in layered memory-defect test matrix. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Build and run representative tests with AddressSanitizer and LeakSanitizer, symbols, fatal findings, and supported-target notes.
Treat WebAssembly pointers as offsets into bounded linear memory with copy, growth, lifetime, and host-reference rules.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 1918. steps.title: Build Asan Lsan Evidence evidence · Theory Asan Lsan Evidence
steps.instruction:
For this theory demonstration of cmem-asan-lsan-evidence, write the complete compile-ready C program headed "/* Evidence: cmem-asan-lsan-evidence-101tp1 */" for changed case 1478. It must classify a defect against complementary native tools, reject one changed boundary, print an observable result and invariant, and return nonzero when the invariant fails. Keep instant code within the PicoC 3.2.2 practice subset; do not claim C23, optimizing compiler, sanitizer, ABI, thread, operating-system, FFI, load, or production evidence until its explicit controlled transfer gate passes.
